title: gastridium australe subsp. laxum
intro: Gastridium australe subsp. laxum, also known as the slender oat grass or loose oat grass, is a subspecies of the Gastridium australe plant, which is a type of grass native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and digestive problems due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Gastridium australe subsp. laxum include its ability to relieve coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as its potential to help manage diabetes and high blood pressure. Gastridium australe subsp. laxum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
